action.reasoning: Observation: Iterations 1 and 2 achieved ~1.5% average accuracy (barely above random). Even the current task accuracy is only ~15%. This indicates the model is failing to learn effectively, not just forgetting. Analysis: The previous model used torchvision's ResNet-18 with pretrained ImageNet weight loading. While common, the pretrained BatchNorm statistics and first-layer mismatch for 32x32 inputs may cause pathological training dynamics. Additionally, mean/std buffers were default float64. Decision: 1) Replace model with a clean from-scratch CIFAR ResNet-18 (no pretrained weights, proper float32 normalization). 2) Run a quick overfit sanity-check on 128 task-0 samples to verify the model can memorize before launching full training. 3) If sanity check passes, run a fast 2-epoch-per-task continual training with experience replay to re-establish baseline accuracy and measure iteration time.
action.command:
python -c "
import torch
from model import CIFAR100ResNet

data = torch.load('/data/task_0/train.pt')
imgs = data['images'][:128]
lbls = data['labels'][:128]
print('Data dtype:', imgs.dtype, 'shape:', imgs.shape, 'labels:', lbls.unique())

device = torch.device('cuda')
model = CIFAR100ResNet(num_classes=100).to(device)
opt = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)
crit = torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss()

for i in range(25):
    opt.zero_grad()
    out = model(imgs.to(device))
    loss = crit(out, lbls.to(device))
    loss.backward()
    opt.step()
    acc = (out.argmax(1) == lbls.to(device)).float().mean().item()
    if i % 5 == 0 or i == 24:
        print(f'Overfit epoch {i:02d} loss {loss.item():.4f} acc {acc:.4f}')
" && python train.py --epochs 2 --batch_size 128 --lr 0.1 --num_workers 2 --memory_per_class 20
